name: C3 Glomerulonephritis
summary: A rare kidney disease where part of the immune system, called the complement system, mistakenly damages the kidneys' filtering units.

overview.whatIsIt: PLACEHOLDER TEXT — replace with sourced content. C3 Glomerulonephritis (C3G) is a rare condition in which the complement system, a part of the immune system that normally helps fight infection, becomes overactive and deposits a protein called C3 in the kidneys' filtering units (glomeruli). Over time, this can affect how well the kidneys filter waste from the blood. It is not caused by anything you did, and it is not contagious.
keyPoints: PLACEHOLDER: C3G is rare and affects the kidneys' tiny filtering units.
PLACEHOLDER: It's driven by the complement system, not a typical infection or autoimmune attack.
PLACEHOLDER: Symptoms and progression vary widely from person to person.
PLACEHOLDER: Ongoing care from a nephrologist (kidney specialist) is central to managing it.
